data.title: Jefferies Abandons Bitcoin and Shifts 10% Back into Gold amid Quantum Computing Fears
data.description:
Jefferies (JEF) has completely removed its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) position from its leading "Greed & Fear" model portfolio, a decision driven by new concerns over technological security. The firm's global head of equity strategy, Christopher Wood, announced he is shifting that 10% allocation back into gold and gold-mining stocks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While Bitcoin has been a massive winner for the portfolio since 2020, Wood believes the long-term "store of value" argument is under threat from the rapid advancement of quantum computing.

Christopher Wood Exits the Digital Gold Trade

Since first adding Bitcoin to his portfolio in late 2020, Christopher Wood has been a high-profile institutional supporter of the asset. However, his latest move signals a significant change in heart. Wood explained that while he doesn't expect a price crash tomorrow, the "store of value" concept is now on a "less solid foundation from the standpoint of a long-term pension portfolio." He decided to redistribute the funds equally, putting 5% into gold bullion and 5% into gold mining companies.

Quantum Computers Challenge Cryptographic Safety

The primary driver for this exit is the looming arrival of "cryptographically relevant" quantum machines. Research cited by Wood suggests that as much as 20% to 50% of all circulating Bitcoin, roughly 4 to 10 million coins, could eventually become vulnerable to theft. This risk exists because older Bitcoin addresses and those that have been reused expose public keys, which a powerful enough quantum computer could potentially use to derive the private keys needed to move funds.

Bitcoin Performance Still Outpaces Gold

Despite the strategic exit, the numbers show that Bitcoin was a highly successful investment for the Greed & Fear strategy. Since Wood's initial buy-in on December 17, 2020, Bitcoin has surged by 325%, whereas gold bullion has risen by 145% in the same timeframe. Wood acknowledged this performance but emphasized that for a multi-decade pension view, gold remains the "historically stress tested store of value".

The crypto community is currently split on how urgent this threat really is. While strategists like Wood are moving capital now, many developers argue that we are still 20 to 40 years away from a quantum computer capable of breaking Bitcoin's security. People who support Bitcoin believe the network has more than enough time to upgrade its security systems before quantum computers become a real threat.

However, for the big-money managers at Jefferies, even the tiny chance of a total collapse is enough to make them choose the safety of gold instead.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $95,513.98.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/16/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise as Chip and Bank Stocks Lift Momentum
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were rising early Friday after solid earnings from tech and bank stocks led a rally in major averages the prior day. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.44%, 0.22%, and 0.10%, respectively, at 3:51 a.m. EST on January 16.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher. Chip stocks surged after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) posted strong Q4 results, reviving optimism around the AI trade. TSMC's shares jumped over 4%, while Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) each rose about 2%. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite each climbed around 0.3%, while the Dow advanced a firmer 0.6%.

Moreover, banking stocks boosted market gains, with Goldman Sachs (GS) and Morgan Stanley (MS) delivering strong quarterly results. Goldman shares surged over 4%, while Morgan Stanley climbed nearly 6%.

​Even with Thursday's gains, investors are heading into the end of a busy week on a cautious note. The major indexes are still on track for weekly losses, with the S&P 500 down about 0.3%, the Nasdaq off 0.6%, and the Dow lower by roughly 0.1% so far this week.

There are no key economic reports scheduled for today.

On the earnings front, regional banks including PNC Financial (PNC), Regions Financial (RF), State Street (STT), and others are expected to report today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.17%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.24 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,604 per ounce on Friday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today as lingering concerns over Greenland's independence continued to weigh on sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets mostly fell on January 16 due to ongoing geopolitical tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index dropped 0.29%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.26%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.37%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 0.32%, and the Topix fell 0.28%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AST SpaceMobile Stock (ASTS) Soars on U.S. Missile Defense Contract Slot
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile's (ASTS) shares jumped about 7% early Friday after it secured a "prime contract position" — with the possibility of an open-ended contract — with the Missile Defense Agency of the U.S. Department of Defense.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under such a contract, the Texas-based company will execute a range of duties to support the quick delivery of "innovative capabilities" to soldiers involved in combat operations.

The contract under review is part of an American government program designed to protect the U.S. against missiles, drones, and other forms of threats across space, in the air, on the ground, and in cyberspace. The program is formally called the Missile Defense Agency Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD).

"The selection positions AST SpaceMobile to compete for a wide range of future task orders across research, development, engineering, prototyping, and operations of critical Missile Defense Agency systems that support U.S. national security objectives," AST SpaceMobile explained in a statement.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Google Play to Block Unregistered Crypto Apps in South Korea, Binance and OKX Are at Risk
data.description:
Accessing major offshore crypto platforms is about to get much harder for South Korean Android users. Starting January 28, 2026, Google (GOOGL) will require all cryptocurrency exchange and custodial wallet apps on the Play Store to provide proof that they are officially registered with the country's Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU). This change acts as a digital gatekeeper, effectively blocking any platform that hasn't secured a local Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) license.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Google Play Sets a Strict Deadline

Google's decision isn't a new law from the Korean government, but rather a global push by the tech giant to align its store with local financial rules. By January 28, developers must upload their FIU registration papers to the Google Play Console or risk having their apps hidden from the South Korean market.

  • Who is safe: Non-custodial wallets where users hold their own private keys (like MetaMask) are generally exempt.
  • Who is at risk: Any exchange that manages user funds but lacks a local Korean office and ISMS security certification.
  • The outcome: New downloads will be blocked, and existing users may lose access to critical updates, eventually making the apps unusable.

It Is a Hard Road to Local Registration

For an international exchange to stay on the Play Store, they must jump through several hurdles set by the FIU. This includes setting up a physical local entity, passing strict anti-money laundering (AML) checks, and getting certified for information security management. While Binance holds a small stake in the local exchange Gopax, it still lacks the formal, direct entity status required to satisfy these new digital storefront rules.

Parliament Approves Tokenized Securities

Interestingly, this crackdown on global apps comes just as South Korea is opening its arms to new blockchain tech on the stock market. The National Assembly just passed a massive set of changes to the Electronic Securities Act and the Capital Markets Act.

These new laws officially recognize tokenized securities, basically turning real-world assets like art, real estate, or investment contracts into digital tokens that can be traded on regulated exchanges. It shows that while the government wants to control who is selling crypto, they are very interested in using the technology behind it to modernize their financial system.

Key Takeaway

To put it simply, South Korea is trying to clean up the neighborhood by making sure every crypto company playing in their yard follows the local rules. It's a bit of a double-edged sword: on one hand, it makes things safer for regular people by keeping out unregulated platforms. On the other hand, it cuts off access to some of the biggest and most popular tools in the world. It feels like the country is building a walled garden, inviting the tech in but keeping the global wild west out.

Investors can track the prices of their favorite cryptos on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: HP Inc. Stock (HPQ) Drops 3% Pre-Market as Analysts Downgrade Rating to Sell
data.description:
HP Inc. (HPQ) shares slipped about 3% in Friday's pre-market trading after several analyst downgrades raised concerns about the company's outlook. The latest move came from Barclays analyst Tim Long, who cut his rating on HPQ to Sell from Hold and slashed his price target to $18 from $24.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Long said that even though HP shares look cheap at about 7x earnings, the company is facing deep, structural challenges across both its PC and printing businesses. He pointed to weak demand, a slowing PC upgrade cycle, and a tough memory pricing scenario that could weigh heavily on HP's consumer PC segment, which makes up about 30% of its Personal Systems division.

The analyst also cut his valuation multiple on HPQ stock, using a 6x P/E (down from 8x) on an unchanged FY27 EPS estimate of $3.03. Long said the lower multiple seems "reasonable," given the current tough memory cycle and the lack of near-term catalysts that could lift the stock.

CFRA and Goldman Join Bearish Trend

The downgrade adds to a growing list of bearish calls on HP this week. CFRA cut its rating to Sell from Hold, assigning a $19 price target. Earlier this week, Goldman Sachs assumed coverage with a Sell rating and a $21 target.

The firm noted that HP has a strong shareholder return strategy, aiming to give back 100% of its free cash flow and maintain solid market share in PCs and printing. But Goldman warned that HP is the most exposed company in its coverage to long-term pressure on PC margins and demand. The firm also sees potential downside to revenue and earnings estimates for Fiscal 2026 and 2027.

Is HPQ a Buy or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Sell consensus rating on HPQ stock based on eight Holds and six Sells assigned in the past three months. Further, the average HP price target of $24.01 per share implies 16.52% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
